name: swiftui-liquid-glass
description: Implement, review, or improve SwiftUI features using the iOS 26+ Liquid Glass API. Use when asked to adopt Liquid Glass in new SwiftUI UI, refactor an existing feature to Liquid Glass, or review Liquid Glass usage for correctness, performance, and design alignment.
capabilities: Implement glassEffect modifier with custom shapes and tints
Create interactive glass elements that respond to touch
Wrap multiple glass elements in GlassEffectContainer
Add morphing transitions using glassEffectID and @Namespace
Apply glass button styles to SwiftUI buttons
Review existing Liquid Glass code for correctness
author: Dimillian
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with only markdown and Swift code examples. No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or environment variable reads. All 148 static findings are false positives: code block delimiters misidentified as shell backticks, documentation URLs flagged as network risks, and generic UI terms misclassified as security threats.
valueStatement: Creating modern iOS interfaces with Liquid Glass requires understanding Apple's specific APIs and design patterns. This skill provides ready-to-use code patterns and review checklists for implementing glass effects correctly.
userTitle: Implement iOS Liquid Glass UI effects in SwiftUI
limitations: Requires iOS 26 or later for Liquid Glass API
Does not provide custom blur implementations
No fallback code generation for older iOS versions

useCases.title: Add glass effects to new UI
useCases.description: Build modern interfaces with Liquid Glass components that blur and reflect background content

useCases.title: Design glass-based interfaces
useCases.description: Create consistent glass surfaces with proper shapes, tints, and interactive states

useCases.title: Audit glass implementation
useCases.description: Verify Liquid Glass usage follows Apple guidelines for performance and design consistency

promptTemplates.title: Basic Implementation
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding glass effect to a view
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a glassEffect modifier to a SwiftUI Text view with a rounded rectangle shape and interactive tint.

promptTemplates.title: Container Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GlassEffectContainer that holds three icon views with consistent spacing and morphing support.

promptTemplates.title: Button Styling
promptTemplates.prompt: Style a SwiftUI button using the glassProminent button style and add it to a GlassEffectContainer.

promptTemplates.title: Review Task
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this SwiftUI view for proper Liquid Glass usage. Check modifier order, container usage, and iOS 26 availability handling.

bestPractices: Apply glassEffect modifier after layout and visual modifiers
Use GlassEffectContainer when multiple glass elements coexist
Gate Liquid Glass code with #available(iOS 26, *) and provide fallbacks
antiPatterns: Using custom blurs instead of native glassEffect API
Missing availability checks for iOS 26 requirement

faq.question: Which iOS versions support Liquid Glass?
faq.answer: Liquid Glass requires iOS 26 or later. Always use #available(iOS 26, *) checks and provide fallback materials.

faq.question: What shapes can glassEffect use?
faq.answer: Common shapes include .capsule (default), .rect(cornerRadius: CGFloat), and .circle for custom effects.

faq.question: How do I combine multiple glass elements?
faq.answer: Wrap multiple views in GlassEffectContainer to enable morphing, blending, and proper spacing between effects.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains only documentation and code examples. No data is collected, stored, or transmitted.

faq.question: Why is my glass effect not animating?
faq.answer: Ensure you use @Namespace with glassEffectID and wrap view changes in withAnimation for morphing transitions.

faq.question: How is this different from materials?
faq.answer: Liquid Glass offers dynamic blurs, real-time interactivity, and morphing capabilities that standard materials cannot provide.
